docs.title: pulselink-mcp
docs.text:
PulseLink MCP API + MCP Server + A2A Agent for the agent-utilities ecosystem — a typed, action-routed connector.

Official documentation

This site is the canonical reference for pulselink-mcp, maintained alongside every release.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
pulselink-mcp wraps the target service with typed, deterministic MCP tools and an optional Pydantic-AI agent server.

The connector remains inactive when credentials are absent: configure PULSELINK_MCP_URL and PULSELINK_MCP_TOKEN to connect it to an instance.

docs.title: Explore the documentation
docs.text:
• Installation — pip, source, extras, and the prebuilt Docker image.
  • Deployment — run the MCP and agent servers, Docker Compose, Caddy + Technitium.
  • Usage — the MCP tools, the Python client, and the CLI.
  • Backing Platform — deploy the target service with Docker.
  • Overview — the action-routed tool surface and architecture.
  • Concepts — the CONCEPT ID registry.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
This page covers running pulselink-mcp as long-lived servers.

pulselink-mcp ships both an MCP server (console script pulselink-mcp) and an A2A agent server (console script pulselink-agent).

docs.title: Deployment Options
docs.text:
pulselink-mcp exposes its MCP server (console script pulselink-mcp) four ways. Pick the row that matches where the server runs relative to your MCP client, then copy the matching mcp_config.json below.

# Option Transport Where it runs mcp_config.json key 1 stdio stdio client launches a subprocess command 2 Streamable-HTTP (local) streamable-http a local network port command or url 3 Local container / uv stdio or streamable-http Docker / Podman / uv on this host command or url 4 Remote URL streamable-http a remote host behind Caddy url

docs.title: 1. stdio (local subprocess)
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pulselink-mcp": {
      "command": "uvx",
      "args": ["--from", "pulselink-mcp", "pulselink-mcp"],
      "env": {
        "PULSELINK_MCP_URL": "https://service.example.com",
        "PULSELINK_MCP_TOKEN": "your_token"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 2. Streamable-HTTP (local process)
docs.text:
uvx --from pulselink-mcp pulselink-mcp --transport streamable-http --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health        # {"status":"OK"}

Connect to the running process by URL:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pulselink-mcp": { "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp" }
  }
}

docs.title: 3. Local container / uv
docs.text:
Launch a container directly from mcp_config.json (swap docker for podman for a daemonless runtime):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pulselink-mcp": {
      "command": "docker",
      "args": [
        "run", "-i", "--rm",
        "-e", "TRANSPORT=stdio",
        "-e", "PULSELINK_MCP_URL=https://service.example.com",
        "-e", "PULSELINK_MCP_TOKEN=your_token",
        "knucklessg1/pulselink-mcp:latest"
      ]
    }
  }
}

Or run a local streamable-http container and connect by URL:

docker compose -f docker/mcp.compose.yml up -d

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pulselink-mcp": { "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp" }
  }
}

docs.title: 4. Remote URL (deployed behind Caddy)
docs.text:
When the server is deployed remotely and published through Caddy on the internal *.arpa zone, connect with the "url" key — no local process or image required:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pulselink-mcp": { "url": "http://pulselink-mcp.arpa/mcp" }
  }
}

Caddy reverse-proxies http://pulselink-mcp.arpa to the container's :8000 streamable-http listener.

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
docker compose -f docker/mcp.compose.yml up -d      # MCP server only
docker compose -f docker/agent.compose.yml up -d    # MCP + agent

docs.title: Run the A2A agent server
docs.text: pulselink-agent --mcp-config mcp_config.json --web

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pulselink-mcp is a standard Python package and a prebuilt container image.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 – 3.14.
  • A reachable target service instance and access token.

docs.title: From PyPI (recommended)
docs.text: pip install pulselink-mcp

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text: Extra Install Pulls in mcp pip install "pulselink-mcp[mcp]" FastMCP MCP-server runtime (agent-utilities[mcp]) agent pip install "pulselink-mcp[agent]" Pydantic-AI agent + Logfire tracing all pip install "pulselink-mcp[all]" Everything above

docs.title: From source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/Knuckles-Team/pulselink-mcp.git
cd pulselink-mcp
pip install -e ".[all]"

docs.title: Docker
docs.text: docker pull knucklessg1/pulselink-mcp:latest

docs.title: pulselink-mcp — Concept Overview
docs.text: Category: Integration | Ecosystem Role: MCP Server + A2A Agent Built on agent-utilities — the unified AGI Harness.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: PulseLink — keyless open-web & social research source (MCP Server + A2A Server)

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
This project follows the standardized agent-package pattern:

  • Modular Design: split into api/ (client mixins) and mcp/ (action-routed tool modules) for cleaner organization.
  • Dynamic Tool Registration: action-routed dynamic tool tags, strictly lowercase, each togglable with a *TOOL environment flag.
  • A2A Agent Server: a Pydantic-AI graph agent (console script pulselink-agent) that calls the MCP tool surface and exposes an AG-UI web interface.

docs.title: Concept Registry
docs.text:
This project implements or inherits the following ecosystem concepts:

Concept ID Description Source ECO-4.1 MCP & Universal Skills agent-utilities (inherited) ECO-4.2 A2A Network & Consensus agent-utilities (inherited)

📖 Full Registry: See agent-utilities/docs/overview.md for the complete 5-Pillar concept index.

docs.title: Backing Platform — PulseLink MCP
docs.text:
pulselink-mcp is a client of a backing service instance. This page provides a Docker recipe for deploying one locally to serve as the target of PULSELINK_MCP_URL.

Backing-system recipe

Each connector in the ecosystem follows the same convention — a docs/platform.md recipe for the system it integrates with, accompanied by a sample Compose stack. Systems offered only as a managed service have no local recipe.

docs.title: Single-node deployment (Compose)
docs.text:
# docker/platform.compose.yml — replace with the real backing-service recipe
services:
  platform:
    image: REPLACE_ME
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"

docs.title: Usage — API / CLI / MCP
docs.text: pulselink-mcp exposes the same capability three ways: as MCP tools an agent calls, as a Python API you import, and as a CLI.

docs.title: As an MCP server
docs.text: Once deployed, the server registers consolidated, action-routed tool modules. Each module is independently togglable with a *TOOL environment flag.

docs.title: As a Python API
docs.text:
from pulselink_mcp.auth import get_client

api = get_client()        # reads PULSELINK_MCP_URL / PULSELINK_MCP_TOKEN from the environment / .env
status = api.get_system_status()

docs.title: As a CLI
docs.text:
export PULSELINK_MCP_URL="http://localhost:8080"
export PULSELINK_MCP_TOKEN="your_token"
pulselink-mcp --transport stdio
